tweets.title:
Senior Pakistani politician Qamar Zaman Kaira has openly questioned the 12 refugee seats in PoJK, arguing that they undermine the rights of PoJK residents.

Under the current system, refugees from Jammu & Kashmir living in Pakistan elect 12 representatives to the PoJK Legislative Assembly despite residing outside the territory. These legislators can vote on the formation of the PoJK government.

Think of it like residents of one city being allowed to elect councillors who can decide the mayor of another city where they do not even live. The local population may vote, but the final outcome can still be shaped by people outside the territory.

This highlights how PoJK residents do not fully control their own assembly, as through refugee constituencies, the Pakistani establishment can influence government formation.

This mechanism provides Islamabad a strategic leverage over PoJK.

tweets.title:
The U.S. Navy plans to deploy up to 8 next-generation Columbia-class nuclear ballistic missile submarines to the Pacific by 2032, significantly strengthening its strategic deterrence posture against China.

Key points:
- Replacing ageing Ohio-class SSBNs
- Designed for lower acoustic signature & higher survivability
- Equipped with 16 Trident II D5LE SLBMs
- 42-year reactor life without refuelling
- Expected to carry ~70% of deployed U.S. nuclear warheads

The move highlights intensifying undersea strategic competition in the Pacific, as China develops the Type-096 SSBN and Russia expands its Borei-class fleet.

Nuclear Attack Submarines (SSNs): The Future of Undersea Warfare 

Unlike SSBNs, which carry strategic nuclear missiles, SSNs are hunter-killers designed to: 
• Track enemy submarines 
• Escort carrier strike groups 
• Gather intelligence 
• Project power far from home waters

Why SSNs Matters ?

SSNs combine unlimited endurance, high speed, and exceptional stealth, allowing them to operate undetected across vast oceans. Their versatility makes them indispensable for surveillance, strike missions, fleet protection, and maintaining blue-water naval dominance.

India's SSN Ambition

India's Arihant-class SSBNs provide strategic deterrence, but future indigenous SSNs will strengthen the Indian Navy's ability to counter adversary submarines, protect carrier battle groups, and maintain a persistent presence across the Indian Ocean Region and beyond.

The Sindhughosh-Class 
The INS Sindhughosh class (also known as Kilo-class) are Russian-built, diesel-electric attack submarines. They are optimized for anti-submarine and anti-shipping operations in shallow littoral waters. Known for their quiet operations, they are armed with heavyweight torpedoes and Klub-S anti-ship cruise missiles. 

Active Submarines 
INS Sindhuraj (S57) 
INS Sindhuratna (S59) 
INS Sindhukesari (S60) 
INS Sindhukirti (S61) 
INS Sindhuvijay (S62)
INS Sindhurashtra (S65) 
INS Sindhushastra (S65) 

Many naval analysts referred to them as "Black Holes" because of the difficulty in detecting them underwater.

Known for their quiet operations, they are armed with heavyweight torpedoes and Klub-S anti-ship cruise missiles.

Active Submarines
INS Sindhuraj (S57)
INS Sindhuratna (S59)
INS Sindhukesari (S60)
INS Sindhukirti (S61)
INS Sindhuvijay (S62)
INS Sindhurashtra (S65)
INS Sindhushastra (S65)

Many naval analysts referred to them as "Black Holes" because of the difficulty in detecting them underwater.
